info.name: joe-404/laravel-auth
info.description:
Complete API collection for the joe-404/laravel-auth Laravel library.

**Two ready-to-use folders:**
- **Web & SPA — Session / Cookie** — for browser/SPA clients. Uses Laravel session cookies + CSRF token. No Authorization header needed on authenticated requests. A folder-level pre-request script automatically fetches `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` before every request in this folder.
- **Mobile — Bearer Token** — for mobile apps and API clients. Uses `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` on every authenticated request. Sends `X-Client-Type: mobile` on login to get a token + refresh token.

**Quick start (Web/SPA mode):**
1. Set `base_url` variable to your app URL
2. Run **Setup → Get CSRF Cookie** once
3. Call **Web & SPA / Registration / 1. Initiate Registration** (email only — no password)
4. Verify email via **2a. Verify OTP** or **2b. Verify Magic Link** — this saves a `completion_token`
5. Call **3. Complete Registration** with the password to finalize the account
6. Then **Login** — session cookie is stored automatically by Postman's cookie jar

**Quick start (Mobile mode):**
1. Set `base_url` variable
2. Call **Mobile / Registration / 1. Initiate Registration** (email only)
3. Verify email — saves `completion_token`
4. Call **3. Complete Registration** — `auth_token` and `refresh_token` are saved automatically
5. Use `POST /auth/token/refresh` before token expires

**Three-step registration:**
The library now uses a 3-step registration flow. The user's password is NEVER sent to `POST /auth/register`. Instead:
1. `POST /auth/register` with `{email}` only → returns `temp_token`
2. Verify email (OTP or magic link) → returns `completion_token` (UUID, 15 min TTL)
3. `POST /auth/register/complete` with `{completion_token, password, password_confirmation}` → creates the user, fires `EmailVerified`, logs them in

**Variables:**
- `base_url` — your Laravel app URL (e.g. `http://localhost:8000`)
- `auth_token` — auto-saved on login / register-complete (mobile) / Google OAuth
- `refresh_token` — auto-saved on login / register-complete (mobile) / token refresh
- `xsrf_token` — auto-saved by SPA folder pre-request script
- `temp_token` — auto-saved after registration initiation
- `completion_token` — auto-saved after email verification (step 2); consumed in step 3
- `reset_token` — auto-saved after magic link password reset step
- `api_token_id` — auto-saved after creating an API token
- `api_token_raw` — auto-saved after creating an API token
- `session_id` — auto-saved after listing sessions

item.name: Setup
item.description: Run these once before using the Web & SPA folder.

request.description:
**Run this before making any Web/SPA mode requests.**

Sanctum sets two cookies:
- `laravel_session` — your session identifier
- `XSRF-TOKEN` — the CSRF token

The test script saves the token to `xsrf_token`. The Web & SPA folder's pre-request script also does this automatically before every request.

**Not required for the Mobile / Bearer Token folder.**

item.name: Web & SPA — Session / Cookie
item.description:
All requests configured for browser/SPA clients.

**How it works:**
- A folder-level pre-request script fetches `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` automatically before every request and saves the token to `xsrf_token`.
- All mutating requests (POST/DELETE/PATCH) include `X-XSRF-TOKEN: {{xsrf_token}}`.
- Authenticated requests use the session cookie set by Postman's cookie jar — no Authorization header needed.
- Login and Complete Registration return `token: null`; Sanctum sets the session cookie on the response.

**Required:** `AUTH_MODE=web` or `AUTH_MODE=both` (without `AUTH_SPA_TOKEN=true`).

exec: pm.sendRequest({ url: baseUrl + '/sanctum/csrf-cookie', method: 'GET' }, function(err, res) {

request.description:
**Step 1 of 3.** Initiates registration. Body is `{email}` only — **NO password** is sent at this step. Sends OTP + magic link to the email (one combined email when `AUTH_VERIFICATION_METHOD=both`).

Does **not** create the user yet — that happens in step 3 after the password is set.

Auto-saves `temp_token` for Reverb channel subscription. After verifying the email (step 2) you will receive a `completion_token` to use in step 3.

**Extra fields example** — if your app collects more data at signup, add them here (still no password):
```json
{
  "email": "joe@example.com",
  "phone": "+96170000000",
  "birthday": "1990-01-01"
}
```

**Errors:** 409 already registered · 422 validation · 429 rate limit

response.name: 201 — Verification email sent
409 — Email already registered
422 — Validation error
429 — Rate limited

request.description:
**Step 2 of 3.** Returns a `completion_token` (UUID, 15 min TTL). Use it in step 3 to set your password and finalize the account.

Replace `123456` with the actual OTP from the email.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid or expired

response.name: 201 — Email verified, completion_token issued
422 — Invalid OTP

request.description:
**Step 2 of 3 (alternative).** Returns a `completion_token` (UUID, 15 min TTL). Use it in step 3 to set your password and finalize the account.

Paste the full magic link URL from the verification email. The library validates the signature.

When `AUTH_MAGIC_LINK_TARGET=frontend`, your frontend receives `?token=uuid` and calls this endpoint with the token.

**Errors:** 422 invalid/expired signature or token already used

response.name: 201 — Email verified, completion_token issued
422 — Invalid or expired signature

exec: console.log('SPA mode — session cookie set, no token returned.');

request.description:
**Step 3 of 3.** Finalizes registration by setting the user's password. The `completion_token` is consumed (one-time use). On success the user is created, assigned the default role, logged in, and the `EmailVerified` event fires.

In SPA mode the response `token` is `null` — Sanctum sets the session cookie.

**Errors:** 422 completion_token invalid/expired · 422 password validation

response.name: 201 — Registration complete (SPA)
422 — completion_token invalid
422 — Password validation

request.description: Resends the verification OTP/link for a pending (unverified) registration. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

response.name: 200 — Sent (or pretended)
429 — Rate limited

exec: console.log('SPA mode — session cookie set by Sanctum. No token returned.');

request.description:
Logs in and receives a session cookie. The response `token` is `null` — Sanctum sets `laravel_session` and `XSRF-TOKEN` cookies that Postman stores automatically.

**Important:** This request intentionally has no `Accept: application/json` header. Sending that header can trigger token mode in `AUTH_MODE=both`. Remove it so the server sees a browser-like request.

**Errors:** 401 wrong credentials/locked · 403 inactive/unverified · 422 validation · 429 rate limit

response.name: 200 — Logged in (SPA)
401 — Wrong credentials
403 — Account not verified
423 — Account locked

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile, roles, permissions, and active session count. Authenticated via session cookie — no Authorization header needed.

response.name: 200 — Profile
401 — Unauthenticated

request.description: Revokes the current session only. The session cookie is invalidated.

response.name: 200 — Logged out

request.description: Revokes ALL sessions and tokens for this user across every device.

response.name: 200 — All sessions revoked

request.description: Sends OTP and/or magic link for password reset. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

response.name: 200 — Reset link sent (or pretended)

request.description:
Step 1 of 2 for OTP-based password reset. Validates the OTP sent to the user's email and returns a short-lived reset_token (15 min). Use the reset_token in **2c. Confirm Reset** to set the new password.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid, 422 OTP expired, 422 validation

response.name: 200 — OTP verified, reset_token issued
422 — OTP invalid
422 — OTP expired
422 — Validation error (missing field)

request.description:
Paste the full URL from the password reset email. Validates the signature and returns a `reset_token` valid for 15 minutes.

Auto-saves `reset_token` to collection variables. Then call **2c. Confirm Reset**.

response.name: 200 — Magic link valid
422 — Invalid signature

exec: console.log('Password reset complete. Session cookie set by server.');

request.description:
Final step of password reset. Accepts the reset_token from Step 2a or 2b and sets a new password.

- **logout_all: true** (default) — revoke all existing sessions/tokens before logging in
- **logout_all: false** — keep existing sessions, just update the password and log in

The user is automatically logged in after a successful reset. Response mirrors the login response: Bearer token for mobile/API clients, session cookie for web/SPA clients.

**Errors:** 422 reset_token invalid/expired, 422 validation

response.name: 200 — Reset + auto-login (logout_all: true)
200 — Reset + auto-login (logout_all: false, keep sessions)
422 — reset_token invalid or expired
422 — Validation error

request.description:
Changes password for the currently authenticated user.

**`logout_all: true`** — revokes all other sessions except the current one.

**Errors:** 422 current_password incorrect or validation failed

response.name: 200 — Password changed
422 — Current password incorrect

request.description:
Returns ALL active sessions for the authenticated user — the current session and every other logged-in device — sorted by most recently active. Each entry includes platform, browser, OS, device info, IP, geo location, and an is_current flag marking which session belongs to this request.

The test script auto-saves a non-current session_id for use in the Revoke request below.

**Errors:** 401 unauthenticated

response.name: 200 — All sessions (current + others)
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Sessions retrieved.",
  "data": {
    "sessions": [
      {
        "id": 3,
        "platform": "mobile",

        "os": "Android",

        "city": "Beirut",

      {

        "device_code": null,
        "device_platform": null,
        "ip_address": "192.168.1.20",
        "city": "Paris",
        "country": "FR",
        "last_active_at": "2026-05-14T18:30:00.000000Z",
        "created_at": "2026-05-14T10:00:00.000000Z",
        "is_current": false
      },
      {
        "id": 1,
        "platform": "web",
        "browser": "Chrome",
        "os": "Windows",
        "device_model": null,

response.name: 401 — Unauthenticated

request.description: Revokes the session with the given ID. Also revokes the associated Sanctum token if it was a token-based session.

response.name: 200 — Session revoked
404 — Not found

request.description:
Lists all third-party API tokens owned by the authenticated user.

**Requires:** `AUTH_MODE=api` or `AUTH_MODE=both`.

response.name: 200 — Tokens list

request.description:
Creates a new third-party API token. The `raw_token` in the response is shown **only once** — store it securely.

**`abilities`:** array of strings. Use `["*"]` for full access.
**`expires_in_days`:** `null` = never expires. Max 3650.

Token format: `auth_at_{base64}`

Auto-saves `api_token_raw` and `api_token_id`.

response.name: 201 — Token created

request.description: Revokes the API token with the given ID. Uses `api_token_id` saved by the Create request.

response.name: 200 — Token revoked

request.description:
Returns the Google OAuth authorization URL. Copy `data.redirect_url` and open it in a browser to start the OAuth flow.

**Requires:** `AUTH_GOOGLE_ENABLED=true`

**Errors:** 403 Google OAuth disabled

response.name: 200 — Redirect URL
403 — Google OAuth disabled

request.description:
Google redirects the user's browser here after authorization. In practice you do not call this directly — Google's redirect handles it.

**Handles 3 cases automatically:** existing provider link · email match · new user.

**Errors:** 403 disabled or inactive · 400 OAuth exchange failed

response.name: 200 — Logged in with Google
202 — Requires link confirmation
400 — OAuth exchange failed

item.name: Mobile — Bearer Token
item.description:
All requests configured for mobile apps and API clients.

**How it works:**
- Login sends `X-Client-Type: mobile` to receive a Bearer token even in `AUTH_MODE=both`.
- Every authenticated request includes `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}`.
- No CSRF cookie or session cookie needed.
- A `refresh_token` is issued alongside the access token — use **Token Refresh** to rotate them before expiry.
- Step 3 of registration (Complete) returns the access + refresh tokens; both are auto-saved.

**Required:** `AUTH_MODE=api` or `AUTH_MODE=both`.

request.description:
**Step 1 of 3.** Initiates registration. Body is `{email}` only — **NO password** is sent at this step. Sends OTP + magic link to the email.

Does **not** create the user yet — that happens in step 3 after the password is set.

Auto-saves `temp_token` for Reverb channel subscription. After verifying the email (step 2) you will receive a `completion_token` to use in step 3.

**Extra fields example** (still no password):
```json
{
  "email": "joe@example.com",
  "phone": "+96170000000",
  "birthday": "1990-01-01"
}
```

**Errors:** 409 already registered · 422 validation · 429 rate limit

response.name: 201 — Verification email sent
409 — Email already registered
422 — Validation error

request.description:
**Step 2 of 3.** Returns a `completion_token` (UUID, 15 min TTL). Use it in step 3 to set your password and finalize the account.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid or expired

response.name: 201 — Email verified, completion_token issued
422 — Invalid OTP

request.description:
**Step 2 of 3 (alternative).** Returns a `completion_token` (UUID, 15 min TTL). Use it in step 3 to set your password and finalize the account.

Paste the full magic link URL from the verification email.

**Errors:** 422 invalid/expired or already used

response.name: 201 — Email verified, completion_token issued
422 — Invalid signature

request.description:
**Step 3 of 3.** Finalizes registration by setting the user's password. The `completion_token` is consumed (one-time use). On success the user is created, assigned the default role, logged in, and the `EmailVerified` event fires.

In Mobile mode the response includes `token` (access) and `refresh_token`. Both are auto-saved.

**Errors:** 422 completion_token invalid/expired · 422 password validation

response.name: 201 — Registration complete (Mobile)
422 — completion_token invalid
422 — Password validation

request.description: Resends OTP/magic link for a pending registration. Always returns 200.

response.name: 200 — Sent (or pretended)

header.description: Optional — enables detailed device tracking in auth_sessions_extended

request.description:
Logs in and returns a Bearer `token` (access) + `refresh_token`. Both are auto-saved to collection variables.

`X-Client-Type: mobile` forces token mode in `AUTH_MODE=both`.

Enable `X-Device-Info` to track device model, platform, and OS version in the session record.

**Errors:** 401 wrong credentials/locked · 403 inactive/unverified · 422 validation · 429 rate limit

response.name: 200 — Logged in (Mobile)
401 — Wrong credentials
403 — Account not verified

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile, roles, permissions, and active session count.

response.name: 200 — Profile
401 — Unauthenticated

request.description: Revokes the current Bearer token and its associated session record.

response.name: 200 — Logged out

request.description: Revokes ALL tokens and sessions for this user across every device.

response.name: 200 — All revoked

request.description:
Exchanges the current `refresh_token` for a new access token + refresh token pair. Both old tokens are revoked immediately.

Call this before the access token expires to keep the user logged in without re-entering credentials. The new tokens are auto-saved to `auth_token` and `refresh_token`.

Token lifetimes are configured per client type in `config/auth_system.php` under `token_ttl`.

**Errors:** 401 refresh token invalid, expired, or already used · 429 rate limit

response.name: 200 — Tokens rotated
401 — Refresh token invalid

request.description: Sends OTP and/or magic link for password reset. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

response.name: 200 — Sent (or pretended)

request.description:
Step 1 of 2 for OTP-based password reset. Validates the OTP sent to the user's email and returns a short-lived reset_token (15 min). Use the reset_token in **2c. Confirm Reset** to set the new password.

**Errors:** 422 OTP invalid, 422 OTP expired, 422 validation

response.name: 200 — OTP verified, reset_token issued
422 — OTP invalid
422 — OTP expired
422 — Validation error (missing field)

request.description: Paste the full URL from the password reset email. Returns a `reset_token` valid for 15 minutes. Auto-saved to collection variables.

response.name: 200 — Magic link valid

exec: console.log('Logged in after reset. auth_token saved.');

request.description:
Final step of password reset. Accepts the reset_token from Step 2a or 2b and sets a new password.

- **logout_all: true** (default) — revoke all existing sessions/tokens before logging in
- **logout_all: false** — keep existing sessions, just update the password and log in

The user is automatically logged in after a successful reset. Response mirrors the login response: Bearer token for mobile/API clients, session cookie for web/SPA clients.

**Errors:** 422 reset_token invalid/expired, 422 validation

response.name: 200 — Reset + auto-login (logout_all: true)
200 — Reset + auto-login (logout_all: false, keep sessions)
422 — reset_token invalid or expired
422 — Validation error

request.description:
Changes password for the currently authenticated user.

**`logout_all: true`** — revokes all other tokens except the current one.

**Errors:** 422 current_password incorrect or validation failed

response.name: 200 — Password changed

request.description:
Returns ALL active sessions for the authenticated user — the current session and every other logged-in device — sorted by most recently active. Each entry includes platform, browser, OS, device info, IP, geo location, and an is_current flag marking which session belongs to this request.

The test script auto-saves a non-current session_id for use in the Revoke request below.

**Errors:** 401 unauthenticated

response.name: 200 — All sessions (current + others)
response.body:

  "success": true,

request.description: Revokes the session with the given ID and its associated Sanctum token.

request.description:
Lists all third-party API tokens owned by the authenticated user.

**Requires:** `AUTH_MODE=api` or `AUTH_MODE=both`.

response.name: 200 — Tokens list

request.description:
Creates a new third-party API token. The `raw_token` is shown **only once** — store it securely.

**`abilities`:** array of strings. Use `["*"]` for full access.
**`expires_in_days`:** `null` = never expires. Max 3650.

Token format: `auth_at_{base64}`

Auto-saves `api_token_raw` and `api_token_id`.

item.name: Use API Token on a Protected Route

request.description:
Example of calling a route protected by `auth.api-token` middleware. Replace the URL with your actual endpoint.

The middleware validates:
1. Token starts with `auth_at_`
2. SHA-256 hash matches a stored record
3. Token is active and not expired
4. Token has the required abilities

response.name: 200 — OK
401 — Token invalid

request.description:
Returns the Google OAuth authorization URL. Open `data.redirect_url` in a browser to start the OAuth flow.

**Requires:** `AUTH_GOOGLE_ENABLED=true`

**Errors:** 403 Google OAuth disabled

response.name: 200 — Redirect URL

exec: console.log('auth_token saved from Google OAuth:', body.data.token);

request.description:
Google redirects the user's browser here after authorization. In practice you do not call this directly.

**Handles 3 cases automatically:** existing provider link · email match · new user.

Auto-saves `auth_token` and `refresh_token`.

**Errors:** 403 disabled or inactive · 400 OAuth exchange failed

response.name: 200 — Logged in with Google
202 — Requires link confirmation

item.name: API Token Client
item.description:
API tokens (format: auth_at_...) are long-lived, scoped keys for third-party integrations such as CI/CD pipelines, scripts, and external services. Completely separate from Sanctum session tokens.

Workflow:
  1. Login with Sanctum → get auth_token
  2. Create an API token → receive api_token_raw (shown ONCE only, save it)
  3. Use api_token_raw as Bearer on routes protected by auth.api-token middleware
  4. Revoke when done

Requires AUTH_API_TOKENS_ENABLED=true in .env

item.name: User — Manage Own Tokens
item.description: Authenticated user managing their own API tokens. Authenticates with Sanctum Bearer (auth_token).

request.description: Returns all API tokens owned by the authenticated user. Raw tokens are never returned — only metadata (id, name, abilities, expiry, last_used_at).

response.name: 200 — Token list
200 — Empty list
401 — Unauthenticated

request.description:
Creates a new API token (auth_at_... format). The raw_token is shown ONLY once — save it immediately.

abilities: array of permission strings. Use ["*"] for full access.
expires_in_days: null for a non-expiring token.

response.name: 201 — Token created (with expiry)
201 — Token created (no expiry)
422 — Validation error
401 — Unauthenticated

item.name: Use API Token on a Protected Route

request.description:
Uses the api_token_raw (auth_at_... format) as a Bearer token to authenticate against a route protected by auth.api-token middleware.

In your host app:
  ->middleware("auth.api-token")             // any valid token
  ->middleware("auth.api-token:read:deploy") // must have specific abilities

Replace /your-protected-route with a real route in your app.

response.name: 200 — Authenticated successfully
401 — No API token in header
401 — Token invalid or expired
403 — Missing required ability

request.description: Revokes an API token by ID. Only the owner can revoke their own tokens. api_token_id is auto-saved by the Create request.

response.name: 200 — Token revoked
404 — Token not found or not yours
401 — Unauthenticated

item.name: Admin — Manage All Tokens
item.description: Admin-only endpoints. Requires super-admin or admin role.

request.description: Admin only — lists every API token across all users and system accounts. Requires super-admin or admin role.

response.name: 200 — All tokens
403 — Insufficient role
401 — Unauthenticated

request.description:
Admin only — creates a system-level token not tied to any user. Useful for service-to-service authentication.

Use ["*"] for all abilities.

response.name: 201 — System token created
403 — Insufficient role

request.description: Admin only — updates abilities or expiry of any token. Omit a field to keep its current value. Set expires_in_days: null to make a token never expire.

response.name: 200 — Token updated
404 — Token not found
403 — Insufficient role

request.description: Admin only — revokes any token regardless of owner.

response.name: 200 — Token revoked
403 — Insufficient role
401 — Unauthenticated

item.name: Reference
item.description: Response format examples and HTTP status code reference.

response.name: 200 — Success envelope

request.description:
Every error response follows this shape:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Human readable error.",
  "errors": { }
}
```

**HTTP status codes used:**
- `200` OK
- `201` Created
- `400` Bad Request
- `401` Unauthorized
- `403` Forbidden
- `404` Not Found
- `409` Conflict (email already registered)
- `422` Unprocessable Entity (validation)
- `423` Locked (account lockout)
- `429` Too Many Requests (rate limit)

response.name: 401 — Error envelope
422 — Validation envelope
